items.title: Soybeans and Corn Extend Gains as US-Iran Hostilities Escalate
items.description: Soybeans and corn futures extended gains as crude oil jumped after hostilities in the Middle East escalated, boosting the appeal of biofuels.

items.title: Tungsten Miner’s Stock Jumps as Billionaire Forrest Buys Stake
items.description: EQ Resources Ltd. shares surged after mining billionaire Andrew Forrest agreed to acquire a 16.8% stake in the Sydney-listed tungsten producer from Oaktree Capital Management LP.

items.title: Gold Steadies as Traders Assess Rates Path After US-Iran Clashes
items.description: Gold steadied after the US and Iran ramped up attacks over the weekend, as traders assess the likelihood of the Federal Reserve raising interest rates to contain inflation.

items.title: Korea President’s Stock Dream Bumps Into Leveraged ETF Backlash
items.description: South Korea’s most closely watched stock-market innovation is morphing into a headache for President Lee Jae Myung.

items.title: Aussie’s Slide Versus Kiwi to Slow on Rate Gap, Strategists Say
items.description: The Australian dollar’s steep selloff against its New Zealand counterpart is set to slow, with analysts saying Australia’s higher interest rates and a key technical support will cushion the currency.

items.title: Bond Traders Take Warsh at Word, See Inflation Fight Continuing
items.description: Bond traders and Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh are in agreement on a crucial point: The central bank’s fight against inflation still seems far from over.

items.title: Tropical Storm Watches Posted Across Florida’s Panhandle Region
items.description: A tropical storm watch has been issued for the Florida Panhandle as the Atlantic’s next event takes shape off shore.

items.title: Caspian Pipeline Consortium Halts Loadings on Drone Strike
items.description: Oil loading at the Caspian Pipeline Consortium’s terminal on Russia’s Black Sea coast was suspended after a drone strike, prompting the Kazakh government to call for an immediate end to such attacks.

items.title: US Strikes Iran in Escalating Campaign After Troops Killed
items.description: The US and Iran engaged in a quickening series of tit-for-tat attacks, with the American military conducting a wave of strikes on the Islamic Republic following the deaths of service personnel.
